name: TempoFiller MCP Server
description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for managing Tempo worklogs in JIRA. Supports both Atlassian Cloud and self-hosted Jira instances. Enables AI assistants to retrieve, create, bulk-create, and delete worklogs, and check work schedules.

tempo_base_url.title: Tempo Base URL
tempo_base_url.description: Tempo API base URL. For Atlassian Cloud use: https://api.tempo.io — For self-hosted Jira use your Jira instance URL (e.g., https://jira.company.com)

tempo_pat.title: Tempo Personal Access Token
tempo_pat.description: Your Tempo Personal Access Token. In Tempo: Settings → API Integration → New Token

jira_base_url.title: Jira Base URL (Cloud only)
jira_base_url.description: Your Atlassian Cloud Jira instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.atlassian.net). Leave blank for self-hosted Jira.

jira_email.title: Jira Email (Cloud only)
jira_email.description: Your Atlassian account email address. Required when using Atlassian Cloud.

jira_api_token.title: Jira API Token (Cloud only)
jira_api_token.description: Your Atlassian API token. Generate one at: https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens. Required when using Atlassian Cloud.

tempo_default_hours.title: Default Hours Per Day
tempo_default_hours.description: Default number of hours to log per day when not specified

tools.name: get_worklogs
tools.description: Retrieve worklogs for authenticated user and date range

tools.name: get_schedule
tools.description: Retrieve work schedule for authenticated user
